heading: Relief that may be granted upon filing petition for recognition
label: 1519 Relief that may be granted upon filing petition for recognition
notes: Effective DateSection effective 180 days after , and not applicable with respect to cases commenced under this title before such effective date, except as otherwise provided, see , set out as an Effective Date of 2005 Amendment note under .Apr. 20, 2005section 1501 of Pub. L. 109–8section 101 of this title

children.text: staying execution against the debtor’s assets;

entrusting the administration or realization of all or part of the debtor’s assets located in the United States to the foreign representative or another person authorized by the court, including an examiner, in order to protect and preserve the value of assets that, by their nature or because of other circumstances, are perishable, susceptible to devaluation or otherwise in jeopardy; and
any relief referred to in paragraph (3), (4), or (7) of section 1521(a).

children.label: b
children.text: Unless extended under section 1521(a)(6), the relief granted under this section terminates when the petition for recognition is granted.

children.label: c
children.text: It is a ground for denial of relief under this section that such relief would interfere with the administration of a foreign main proceeding.

children.label: d
children.text: The court may not enjoin a police or regulatory act of a governmental unit, including a criminal action or proceeding, under this section.

children.label: e
children.text: The standards, procedures, and limitations applicable to an injunction shall apply to relief under this section.

children.label: f
children.text: The exercise of rights not subject to the stay arising under section 362(a) pursuant to paragraph (6), (7), (17), or (27) of section 362(b) or pursuant to section 362() shall not be stayed by any order of a court or administrative agency in any proceeding under this chapter.o
